text: All previous bugs are gone

title: Mr
text: This app is incredible. Look no further. I had tried many. It has all the features very intelligently.

title: Love this
text: I have tried many different dictation apps and this is, without a doubt, the best one. It works simply, it works well, and it works with every app on my MacBook.

title: Updates have really slowed down
text: The app had great potential when it launched, but it seems that the developer has lost active interest. It has hardly added any new features for over a year.

title: This App Is Perfect
text: It does exactly what it needs to in order to be hyper useful and practical, and it works flawlessly. An absolute 10/10. Incredible job.

title: The new update makes it rock, starting to beat PLAUD
text: I fell in love with this app when it first came out and just lunged in and bought it for a year or whatever it was. It was good but it tended to rewrite your notes in a way that often left out a little bit of nuance and information. The new version is fantastic you can also go ahead and update notes afterwards as well as you make it incredibly useful, integrated with some automations and workflows this is a fantastic app and kudos to the solo developer who made it. I didn't use it for months when I got PLAUD, it's transcription and options were just so good but that's now falling behind badly, companies that grow that quickly tend to prioritize investors over customers and it's starting to show, the best worst feature of last year was PLAUD showing you are blocking advert 10 seconds before you're allowed to go and use the app, kind of killed its utility

title: Super nifty tool
text:
Effortless, accurate, and surprisingly powerful —
AudioPen  nails voice transcription. It doesn’t just capture what I say — it rewrites it into what I meant to say, trimming the fluff and filler like magic. Way better than voice memos or iOS dictation. Honestly shocked it’s not more popular. Edit: I’m an AudioPen Prime member and just love how easy it is to take notes. My favorite thing about this app though, and what sets it apart from similar apps, is the clean and gorgeous UX design. Everything just feels intuitive and seamless. Love it.

title: Love this app
text: I can’t say enough about AudioPen. I have used it 4-5 days a week for a few years. It helps me capture my thoughts and then tuen them into something that is organized so I can write longer form essays. I like that you can have it to the summary or you can download the full transcript.

title: good but slow
text: I use it quite regularly and it is good, but not good at all for anything quick   takes a long time to process.     other than that I like it!

title: Can’t log in
text: Useless: the first thing I have to do to do anything in the app is enter my email then put in a code from that email. Code never arrives no matter how many times I try. Can’t use the app at all

title: Simple and powerful
text: Amazing at taking my incoherent, nonlinear thoughts and making them usable. Love the UI, reasonably priced, indispensable for any freelancer or ADHD person!

title: Great app
text: It does exactly what it’s supposed to do and been major life saver great tool!

title: I’ve been looking for an app exactly like this for years.
text: It’s the missing link for my workflow and solves so many of the problems I had with other quick capture techniques. Only request, please integrate into the iOS settings that support this being used in Shortcuts to automatically start recording a note (I want to replace my iPhone Action Button with this)

title: Indispensable
text: There are plenty of mediocre and half baked AI apps out there that promise far more than they deliver. audioPen on the other hand, lives up to its promise and delivers every time. I wish more people would use it to clarify their thoughts before they send a request... it is also a big plus when you have it developer who is highly responsive to its users and Louis is clearly committed to making his app the best in class.

title: A favorite tool
text: I’ve been using the web version of AudioPen for about a year now, and it’s so amazing. It’s one of my favorite tools that helps me as a disabled business owner. I’m so stoked about the app, it looks amazing. And knowing the level of care and work that Louis puts into AudioPen, I know I can trust its quality. Congrats, Louis!!!

title: Prolific made simple
text:
Over the past three decades inspiring others for a living, I've had so many great ideas never see the light of day. I’d start organizing and typing with the best of intentions, but then I get stuck.

How AudioPen Changed My Life

The freedom to speak my ideas and have a tool that turns my raw and often rambling thoughts into clear text is a game changer.

I'm grateful to have found AudioPen on ProductHunt when it first launched. Today, I use AudioPen to capture ideas, to journal, and to “speak-write” all kinds of content.

So far, I've used it to author more than 100 essays and 5 digital products. And right now, as I speak this review in September 2024, I'm using AudioPen to write a series of short books I plan to publish on Amazon Kindle.

But this isn't “AI-generated content” any more than having a professional editor clean up my writing would be “editor-generated” content.

Two Ways I Use AudioPen

1. Capturing Ideas
- Created a Shortcut
- Connected to Notion
- Created a custom style

2. Content Creation
- What’s the big idea?
- Mindmap the key points
- Use frameworks to organize

I also use it to brainstorm, create lists, and more.

The secret sauce hidden in AudioPen is the Writing Style or “recipe” feature — making it simple to train it to transform my words in a certain way.

There are stock styles in AudioPen and I have created custom styles for essays, social posts, book chapters, journaling, etc.

Result: The 'style' innovation allows me to write with AI without overthinking and without losing the human voice that makes the writing me.

How Does AudioPen Compare? 

I've tried a half dozen other speech-to-text apps, but none of the others get it right. 

AudioPen is best in class.

That's why it's the primary tool I recommend and teach in my Prolific Workflows training and coaching.

You can probably tell by now that I love AudioPen. And arguably the biggest endorsement is the most coveted place it holds on my iPhone 15: the action button.

In the unlikely event you don't know what that is, Google it, add AudioPen to YOUR action button, and use it daily to get out there, get your message heard, and do all the things you dream about.

All the best.

title: Perfect for voice notes
text: I’ve used AudioPen for almost a year and love it. It’s perfect for long voice notes that I want to turn into a doc I can share.

title: Didn’t work
text: I so wanted this to work but I tried it 3 times and it did nothing at all. Deleted.

author_name: Good job, does what it should
title: Amazing rewrites from messy thoughts
text: I've been using this app for a while and it's amazing. I can talk in a messy and unstructured way and the result is amazing. From daily diary entries to ideas to comments about books, it's great. For me what stands out is the quality of the rewrite

title: Useless
text:
There is nothing free, it actively pushes for people to pay for their pro version..nothing else.
I downloaded it upon suggestion from a workshop ( probably paid by the company), I decided to trial the free version and then eventually move to the paid one if I liked it. The problem is the free version is useless, I made a 2 minutes voice note in plain english and it’s been 40 minutes saying “preparing transcription” and then it does nothing. Every time the phone locks or I receive a notification, it will stop and say “transcription failed, retry”. Complete waste of time. I am deleting my account and the app the second I finish this review. It clearly just pushes for paid version.

title: Best of the bunch
text: There are a lot of similar transcription apps but AudioPen is the most polished and focused. It just needs Shortcuts and a watch app and it's 5*.

title: An absolute game changer
text: Works brilliantly. A superb example of AI being used in order to enhance rather than to threaten human creativity. It really has been a game changer - and creator Louis continues to improve it

title: Doesn’t work
text: Free version doesn’t work. Wasted time trying to use it

title: Love it
text: Best AI transcription app out there, period.

title: AMAZING!
text:
Audiopen has really helped me, professionally & personally! 
I love that it's so versatile and simple to use. 
Totally worth the price!
Additionally, the founder is so prompt to reply to emails whenever the need arises.

title: Awful
text: The app has such a bad experience and it breaks randomly

title: Not a native app
text:
This founder has a history of lying to their customers. This is not a native app and he create fake demand and raise prices. I’m one of those who bought it. Additionally he has a army of followers who retweeted his app but in reality it is mediocre. 

I highly recommend others to try apps like Oasis, Otter, Audio Writer, Voice notes app they are half price as this app and more ethical in terms what they do.

title: Awesome app
text: Siri looks completely outdated once you've tried this app. It lets you turn speech into meaningful, properly formatted text in any field or program — with just one shortcut. It works really well and makes my life a lot easier.

title: Enfin un usage de l’IA où ça fait sens pour moi.
text: Simple et efficace, un outil à portée de main, au quotidien.

title: Utilisation facile prise en main claire
text: J’utilise AudioPen pour mettre en forme mes pensées et organiser mon travail pour mon mémoire. J’aime beaucoup cette application car je peux l’utiliser partout dès que idée me vient. Merci ariane

title: I can’t see my past recordings on my free account
text:
This was a disappointment as I had heard that I would be able to see and use all my past recordings.
Maybe you need to have a Prime account to see them?

title: Excellent tool for clear and efficient transcription
text:
Audiopen has become an invaluable part of my workflow. It converts spoken thoughts into clear, well-structured text with impressive accuracy, saving a great deal of time and effort. The interface is intuitive, the processing is fast, and the output requires minimal editing.

What I especially appreciate is how naturally it captures tone and intent, making the text feel human rather than mechanical. Whether for notes, ideas, or longer reflections, Audiopen consistently delivers reliable results.

Highly recommended for anyone who wants to turn voice into high-quality text quickly and effortlessly.

title: Super useful
text: I use this app all the time. I love how I can just speak for rainbow thoughts, and how it turns it into a well laid out text of my choice. I specifically use it while reading books or listening to podcasts – such a seamless experience.

title: Such a powerful app
text: I have never ever written an app review, but this one is so useful for me and doing a great job. I do my notes on my own language and do a perfect translation for English. I can’t recommend enough who love making notes but don’t like to write or just feel overwhelmed with all the little notes.
